bullets.text: Personal Lines profitability strong despite $1B+ in policyholder credits; reduced accident frequency from COVID driving restrictions drove favorable loss ratios; Commercial Lines CR 84.1, improving 4.3 points YOY as vehicle utilization fell.

Personal auto PIFs up 11% YOY at June 30 (+1.5M policies vs. June 2019); special lines new business applications +22% as consumers sought outdoor recreation; auto PIFs grew despite new application decline due to strong renewal base and billing leniency.

Property NPW up 13% YTD; Direct auto quotes up 6% YOY for Q2 as shopping recovered; Commercial Lines small business insurance BQX applications +36% in quarter; Progressive #1 writer of personal auto in 7 states, #2 in 19 more.

Q2 investment return 4.5% as markets rapidly recovered; conservative portfolio positioning entering COVID crash allowed opportunistic additions at attractive valuations; ARX Holding Corp. (Progressive Home) acquired 100% April 1, completing full ownership.

Vehicle miles driven down ~33% from pre-COVID levels; TNC NPW adjusted down ~$29M as rideshare mileage collapsed; property Q2 CR 143.6 with 54.3 catastrophe CR points from 20 declared events including wind, hail, and tornadoes.

Property 4.0 product launched in 7 more states (18 total); minimum wind/hail deductible requirements added in 6 more states (16 total); Michigan PIP reform product successfully launched in June for state's largest personal auto insurer.

BOP available through agents in 11 states; HomeQuote Explorer Buy launched on mobile devices in 17 states; Portfolio quoting for agents in 37 states; BOP 1.2 with broader risk acceptance in development; Commercial Lines Atlas system investment continued.

43,000+ employees transitioned to remote work in days; IT team enabled mass work-from-home capability; employee financial relief fund established; bonus advance paid in April; $8M Progressive Insurance Foundation donations to hunger, health, and homelessness charities.

Direct channel recovery materially outpaced Agency during Q2; small business insurance BQX direct applications up 36%; $40M+ in agent contingency advances and grants to support independent agent network during pandemic.

COVID shelter-in-place orders created sharp bifurcation in Commercial Lines — for-hire transportation and essential goods trucking recovered quickly while TNC rideshare and non-essential business segments remained severely depressed.
